title: [Test Containers] Support for Ryuk functionality
body:
### Feature request description

Hi!
I am trying to setup automated tests on Jenkins - our jobs are run on Kubernetes Pods with Podman. I want to use Test Contatiners with Java and Selenium.
Test Containers provide small "extra" container that is responsible for other containers lifecycle management making Test Containers easy to use and ready to go almost immediately. However for Podman, this lovely feature must be disabled and there are issues with containers lifecycle.
Locally on Windows11 above setup with Docker desktop works very well.

I also found info that Test Containers don't plan to modify Ryuk specifically for Podman :(
https://github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-java/issues/8167#issuecomment-2133524409



### Suggest potential solution

Would be really cool if Podman worked with Ryuk like docker does, so people don't have to "reinvent the wheel" aka write container lifecycle management code in each project that wants to use TestContatiners+Podman combo.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Unfortunately with my tech stack I don't have many other options :(

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: We do not feel comfortable changing the default right now from `pasta` to `bridge` as that could be considered a breaking change. However, you can override the default in `containers.conf`. Setting `netns` to `bridge` should do what you want.

If they are actually using the docker compat API to create the container then we already default to the bridge mode there but if they shell out to the podman cli then not of course. The config file is always an option as pointed out by @mheon.


If you have other concrete problems please file issues with the exact API payloads used and what they expect to happen with them so we can see if the docker compat API might have some gaps.
